scope.notes: ENTITY ONLY. No project, corridor, route, or treatment_component aliases are included.

proposed_aliases.reason: Both records represent the same global MTA Headquarters entity. The alias has display_name "MTA Headquarters (MTAHQ)", entity_name "MTA Headquarters", acronym "MTAHQ", and office/agency context; the target has display_name and entity_name "MTA Headquarters". The acronym variant is not a distinct organization.
Both records have display_name and entity_name "MTA Headquarters" and record_kind entity. The source difference is only the meeting document where the same headquarters organization was mentioned.

Both records have record_kind entity, display_name "MTA Headquarters", entity_name "MTA Headquarters", and agency context. This is the same global MTA Headquarters organization, not a source-specific entity.

The alias display_name "MTA Headquarters (MTAHQ)" and entity_name "MTA Headquarters" are an acronym-bearing variant of the target "MTA Headquarters" record. Both are entity records for the same MTA headquarters organization.

The alias display_name "MTA Headquarters (MTA HQ)", entity_name "MTA Headquarters", and acronym "MTA HQ" are the same headquarters entity represented by the target "MTA Headquarters". Parenthetical acronym formatting is the only identity difference.

Both records have display_name and entity_name "MTA Headquarters" with entity record kind and agency context. The supporting block for the alias lists "MTA Headquarters" as the same MTA organizational unit.

The alias display_name "MTA HQ", entity_name "MTA Headquarters", and acronym "MTAHQ" are abbreviation variants of the target "MTA Headquarters" entity. The records refer to the same global MTA headquarters organization.
Both records represent the same global MTA Procurement Department. The alias and target share record_kind entity, display_name/entity_name "MTA Procurement Department", and department context.

Both records have display_name/entity_name "MTA Procurement Department", record_kind entity, and department entity_type. The meeting-doc suffix only reflects duplicate extraction from a different source.
Both records represent MTA Procurement Department as an entity department. They share the same display_name/entity_name and differ only by source-specific record ID.

record_existence_and_kind.command: sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "select record_id, record_kind, display_name, json_extract(payload,'$.entity_name') as entity_name, json_extract(payload,'$.entity_type') as entity_type, json_extract(payload,'$.acronym') as acronym, primary_source_id from records where record_id in (...) order by record_id;"
record_existence_and_kind.observed: 13 inspected records existed; all inspected alias/target records returned record_kind entity. The proposed aliases are MTA Headquarters variants and MTA Procurement Department variants only.

commands: { jq -r '.aliases.entity | keys[]?' data/identity-overrides/merges.json; sqlite3 data/canonical.db "select alias from identity_aliases where kind='entity';"; jq -r '.proposed_aliases[]? | select(.kind=="entity") | .alias_id' data/review_notes/codex-identity-batch0{47..99}-prep.json 2>/dev/null; } | sort -u > /tmp/codex100_occupied_entity_aliases.txt

for id in entity_meeting-doc-129051-mtahq entity_meeting-doc-192306-mta-headquarters entity_meeting-doc-199136-headquarters entity_meeting-doc-37086-mtahq entity_meeting-doc-52816-mta-hq entity_meeting-doc-68121-mta-headquarters entity_meeting-doc-mta-hq entity_meeting-doc-113916-mta-procurement entity_meeting-doc-163216-mta-procurement entity_meeting-doc-196901-mta-procurement; do if rg -qx "$id" /tmp/codex100_occupied_entity_aliases.txt; then echo "alias_conflict $id"; fi; done
existing_alias_conflicts.observed: 642 occupied entity aliases were loaded from merges.json, identity_aliases, and prior identity prep artifacts through batch099. Candidate alias conflict command printed no conflicts.

commands: { jq -r '.pairs.entity[]? | .record_ids | sort | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json; sqlite3 -separator $'\t' data/canonical.db "select case when record_id_a < record_id_b then record_id_a else record_id_b end, case when record_id_a < record_id_b then record_id_b else record_id_a end from do_not_merge where kind='entity';"; } | sort -u > /tmp/codex100_entity_dnm_pairs.tsv
candidate alias-target pairs were compared against /tmp/codex100_entity_dnm_pairs.tsv
do_not_merge_conflicts.observed: 20 entity do-not-merge pairs were loaded from data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json and sqlite do_not_merge. Candidate pair conflict check printed no conflicts.

prior_prep_conflicts.observed: 84 prior prepared entity alias IDs were inspected; none match the 10 aliases in this artifact.

source_evidence.command: sqlite3 -header -column data/canonical.db "with first_ref as (...) select first_ref.record_id, first_ref.source_id, first_ref.page_number, first_ref.block_id, substr(blocks_fts.raw_text,1,160) as block_text from first_ref left join blocks_fts on blocks_fts.source_id=first_ref.source_id and blocks_fts.block_id=first_ref.block_id order by first_ref.record_id;"
source_evidence.observed: Evidence refs and block text were inspected locally for the proposed alias/target records. Procurement blocks explicitly include Department MTA Procurement / MTA Procurement Department; MTA Headquarters records have matching payload names and source refs containing MTA HQ, MTAHQ, or MTA Headquarters context.

forbidden_command_compliance.observed: No external/provider-backed commands or profiles/providers were used. No canonicalize, canonicalize-wave, canonicalize-review, campaign dedup, identity-review-run, write, ask, schema-propose, ontology-normalize-run, materialize, export-jsonl, or validate command was run. Inspection used local deterministic commands only: rg, jq, sqlite3 data/canonical.db, sed/head/ls/git status/date, and apply_patch for this prep artifact.
